summary: Test 5 (Eclipse 2026) was a single-paragraph deferral to Section 4.2. EXP-159 PATCH-9 replaces the stub with a three-paragraph self-contained kill-shot: (1) credit for the dome's pre-registered methodology (most methodologically careful element in the registry), (2) the geometric kill-shot that the August 2026 eclipse path is computed from JPL DE440/441 ephemerides on globe Keplerian orbits and the dome supplies no independent Besselian elements, (3) the non-discrimination argument that the magnetic dip formula (baseline x coverage x FSF) is engineered from globe-era BOU baseline and globe-computed coverage and lands inside Chapman 5-25 nT by construction, plus three inconsistent dome values (-17 to -21, -28.9, -29.1 nT). Section 4.2 is still linked for per-station numbers and the four discriminating sub-tests, so this is addition not duplication. Recommend close once patched.
